license: CC0-1.0 — authored for kern, no external corpus text
about: kern's own ground-truth retrieval corpus: 8 sessions, 1-based turn indices (blank-line unit, matching split.rs::paragraph_split and distill.rs::split_turns). Evidence triples are [session_id, turn_1based, anchor-substring]; the anchor must appear in the cited turn — test_eval_ground.py enforces it.

turns: user: Hey, first time using this. I'm Mara, I work as a backend engineer at a logistics startup called Freightwise.
assistant: Nice to meet you, Mara. A backend engineer at Freightwise — noted. What are you working with day to day?
user: Mostly Go services and a big Postgres cluster. I live in Rotterdam, moved here two years ago from Leipzig.
assistant: Rotterdam via Leipzig, Go and Postgres at work. Got it.
user: I have a dog, a whippet named Biscuit. She's three and terrified of thunderstorms.
assistant: Biscuit the whippet, three years old, hates thunderstorms. Anything else about your setup at home?
user: I share a flat near Blaak station with one roommate, Jonas. He's a nurse and works night shifts.
assistant: A flat near Blaak with Jonas, who nurses on night shifts — so quiet mornings matter.
user: Exactly. Also for the record: I'm vegetarian, and I can't stand cilantro.
assistant: Vegetarian, no cilantro. I'll remember both.
user: Work update. I got assigned tech lead on a new project called Kestrel.
assistant: Congratulations — tech lead on Kestrel. What does the project do?
user: It's a route-optimization engine for truck fleets. We promised the pilot customer a demo on April 15th.
assistant: Route optimization for truck fleets, pilot demo April 15th. Tight?
user: Very. We picked Rust for the solver core and kept Go for the API layer.
assistant: Rust solver core, Go API layer — a pragmatic split.
user: The pilot customer is a Dutch carrier called Vos Transport. Their ops lead is named Hanneke.
assistant: Vos Transport, ops lead Hanneke. I'll keep the names straight.
user: My biggest worry is the solver's memory use on the 5000-stop benchmark. It peaks around 11 GB right now.
assistant: 11 GB peak on the 5000-stop benchmark is the number to beat. What's the target?
user: We need it under 4 GB to fit the customer's edge boxes.
assistant: Under 4 GB on edge hardware — that's a real constraint. Worth profiling allocations first.
user: Booking some travel. My sister Elin is getting married in Oslo on June 20th.
assistant: Elin's wedding, Oslo, June 20th — exciting. Flying or taking the train?
user: Flying, but I want to be back by June 23rd because Biscuit's dog-sitter can only cover three days.
assistant: Back by June 23rd for Biscuit's sitter. Who's watching her?
user: Jonas offered but he has shifts, so we hired a sitter named Petra from a neighborhood app.
assistant: Petra the sitter, three days max. Noted.
user: Also planning a hiking trip in the Ardennes with two friends from Leipzig, probably late May.
assistant: Ardennes hike, late May, with the Leipzig friends. Do they have names I should know?
user: Tomas and Ricarda. Tomas is the one who got me into climbing years ago.
assistant: Tomas — climbing origin story — and Ricarda. Got it.
user: Annoying discovery: turns out I'm allergic to hazelnuts. Mild, but my lips swell.
assistant: Hazelnut allergy with lip swelling — mild but worth taking seriously. Diagnosed by a doctor?
user: Yes, allergist confirmed it last Thursday. She also said to carry antihistamines when eating out.
assistant: Allergist-confirmed, antihistamines when eating out. I'll flag hazelnuts alongside your no-cilantro rule.
user: On a better note, I signed up at a bouldering gym called Grip near the harbor. Tuesdays and Fridays.
assistant: Grip bouldering gym, Tuesdays and Fridays. Back into climbing like the Leipzig days?
user: Exactly, Tomas would be proud. My goal is to climb a 6B by the end of summer.
assistant: A 6B by end of summer — solid goal for a returning climber.
user: Also Biscuit needs her booster vaccination in April, the vet sent a reminder for April 8th.
assistant: Biscuit's booster on April 8th. I'll keep that with her file.
user: Big week. We got the Kestrel solver memory down to 3.4 GB on the 5000-stop benchmark.
assistant: 3.4 GB — under the 4 GB edge-box target with room to spare. What did it?
user: Switching the distance matrix to int16 quantization and streaming the stop graph from disk.
assistant: int16 distance matrix plus streaming the stop graph — nice. Demo still on for April 15th?
user: Yes, and Hanneke is bringing Vos Transport's fleet director, so the stakes went up.
assistant: Fleet director in the room on April 15th. High stakes, good position.
user: HR also opened a staff-engineer req. My manager Priya told me to apply.
assistant: Priya's nudging you toward staff engineer — that's a strong signal. Applying?
user: I submitted the internal application yesterday. Panel interview is set for April 22nd.
assistant: Applied, panel on April 22nd. A week after the demo — busy month.
user: Housing news: Jonas is moving in with his partner in June, so I need to decide about the flat.
assistant: Jonas moving out in June — keep the flat solo, find a roommate, or move?
user: I can't afford it alone. I'm leaning toward a one-bedroom in Katendrecht I saw listed.
assistant: Katendrecht one-bedroom over a new roommate. What's the rent situation?
user: 1250 a month, which is 200 more than my half now, but no roommate roulette.
assistant: 1250 versus 1050 — paying 200 for certainty. When would you move?
user: Viewing is Saturday. If it works out, lease would start July 1st.
assistant: Viewing Saturday, lease July 1st. That sequences fine after Elin's wedding.
user: Vet update: Biscuit got her booster on the 8th as planned, all fine, she weighs 12.1 kg.
assistant: Booster done April 8th, 12.1 kg, healthy whippet. Good.
user: The Kestrel demo happened on the 15th. It went well — Vos signed a six-month pilot contract.
assistant: Demo delivered and a six-month pilot signed with Vos. Huge. How did the solver behave?
user: Flawless, 3.2 GB peak and route quality 8 percent better than their current tool.
assistant: 3.2 GB and 8 percent better routes — numbers that close deals. Celebrating?
user: Team dinner Friday. Separately, I joined a book club that meets first Monday of each month.
assistant: Book club, first Monday monthly. What's the first book?
user: The Overstory by Richard Powers. I'm halfway and it's wrecking me in a good way.
assistant: The Overstory will do that. Enjoy the wreckage.
user: I also bought a used acoustic guitar, a Yamaha FG800, to pick playing back up.
assistant: Yamaha FG800 acoustic — welcome back to guitar. Fingers will complain for two weeks.
user: Two updates. First: I got the staff engineer role, effective May 1st.
assistant: Staff engineer, effective May 1st — congratulations, Mara. The panel on the 22nd clearly went well.
user: Second: the Katendrecht viewing worked out, I signed the lease. Moving July 1st, so update my address situation.
assistant: Lease signed — from the Blaak flat to Katendrecht on July 1st. I'll treat Katendrecht as your address from then.
user: Correction on an old thing: Biscuit is actually four now, her birthday was April 20th.
assistant: Biscuit is four as of April 20th — updating from three.
user: The Ardennes hike is locked for May 24th to 26th. Ricarda booked a gite near La Roche-en-Ardenne.
assistant: May 24th to 26th, gite near La Roche-en-Ardenne, booked by Ricarda. Boots ready?
user: Getting there. Also hit my first 6A at Grip yesterday, so the 6B goal feels reachable.
assistant: First 6A down, 6B by summer within reach. Tomas would indeed be proud.
